content.text: Circuit Tracks is a standalone groovebox that combines two polyphonic synth tracks with four sample-based drum tracks, two dedicated MIDI tracks, a powerful 32-step sequencer, and built-in effects. It's designed for making complete tracks without a computer.

content.name: Two Synth Tracks
content.description: Polyphonic synthesizer tracks with 128 factory patches and room for your own sounds via Novation Components.

content.name: Four Sample Tracks
content.description: Sample-based tracks for drums and percussion. Each track can play one sample at a time with independent pitch, amp, and filter controls.

content.name: Two MIDI Tracks
content.description: Dedicated tracks for sequencing external synthesizers and drum machines. Full 32-step sequencing with parameter locks, velocity, and gate length.

content.name: 32-Step Sequencer
content.description: Each track has its own 32-step sequencer with per-step parameter locks, probability, and micro-timing.

content.text: Circuit Tracks receives regular firmware updates that add new features and improvements. Use Novation Components (available as a web app or standalone desktop application) to update firmware, manage patches and samples, back up projects, and access deep editing parameters.

content.title: Staying Up to Date
content.text: Check Novation's website or open Components to see if firmware updates are available. Updates have added features like improved MIDI routing, new effects, and performance enhancements.
The Master Volume knob controls the overall output level for both headphones and line outputs. This is an analog control that doesn't affect recording levels or saved settings.
The velocity-sensitive RGB pads serve multiple purposes depending on the current mode:

items: Note Mode: Play notes chromatically (synth tracks) or trigger samples (drum tracks)
Step Mode: Program the sequencer with note data
Velocity Mode: View and edit velocity values for each step
Length Mode: Adjust gate length for each step
Pattern Mode: Select from 8 patterns per session
Mixer Mode: Adjust track levels visually

content.text: The large Master Filter knob is a DJ-style filter that sweeps across the entire mix output. Turn left for low-pass (bass only), turn right for high-pass (treble only). The center detent position is neutral/bypass.

content.title: Performance Tip
content.text: Use the Master Filter for dramatic transitions between patterns or to create drops and builds during live performance.
Hold the Shift button to access secondary functions. Many buttons have alternate behaviors when Shift is held.

content.title: Essential Shortcuts
content.text: Shift + Octave Down = Undo. Shift + Octave Up = Redo. Shift + Save = Setup menu. These work across most operations.
Use the Octave +/- buttons to shift the keyboard range up or down. The current octave is indicated by the RGB LED colors on the pads when playing notes.
